vector_description: How well the candidate’s degree field matches the required field for the role.
The level of education attained by the candidate (e.g., Bachelor’s, Master’s, PhD).
How closely the candidate’s degree specialization aligns with the job requirements.
Quality of academic performance, including grades, CGPA, ranking, and honors.
Relevance or prestige of the educational institution attended by the candidate.
Presence of job-relevant technical or functional skills in the candidate’s profile.
The depth or mastery level of each relevant skill the candidate possesses.
Number of years the candidate has applied or used a skill in real projects or roles.
Presence of certifications relevant to the job or skill set required.
Credibility and recognition of the organization issuing the certification.
How recently the candidate has worked with required skills, tools, or domains.
How closely the candidate’s previous job titles and domains align with the role.
Length of time the candidate has spent in relevant roles or industries.
Extent of measurable or demonstrated business impact delivered in past roles.
The complexity and scale of projects the candidate has handled.
Level of responsibility or leadership the candidate has reached in past roles.
Consistency and stability of the candidate’s employment history (e.g., frequent moves vs. long stays).
